title: MANGA NGE Tea scene
wikitext:
[]: [NGE] Tea scene?             Quote Post by NeonZ » Fri Aug 20, 2004 5:07 pm Is it in the anime? I'm talking about the scene with Rei making tea to Shinji. Also, when was the manga with that scene released in Japan?

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Aug 20, 2004 5:30 pm No that scene was not in the anime. I have no idea when the manga with that scene was released.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Aug 20, 2004 10:36 pm As previously stated, the tea scene was not in the anime. That stage appears in volume 5, which was published in 1999, so the stage appeared in Young Shonen Ace sometime in 98-99, which means that it came out well beyond the anime, movies, or directors cuts. If you need a specific month, I would suggest sending an email to Eva Otaku which has posted stage translations for quite awhile back.

[]: Yes... but at least it's warm.             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Sat Aug 21, 2004 1:24 am Realy cool scene though.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Aug 21, 2004 2:08 am I think it was very necessary. In the anime, any awkwardness from the aprtment scene in episode 5 between Rei and Shinji is completely gone after episode 6. In the manga however it gets touched upon afterwards, and scenes like this tea scene show that Shinji is still awkward and uncomfortable around Rei like you would think he would be.

[]:             Quote Post by NeonZ » Sat Aug 21, 2004 2:02 pm Thank you for the info. I was just a bit confused because I saw that scene in the game SRW Alpha, even though it's mainly based in the anime, and I couldn't remember watching that scene before, though I did remember seeing it in the manga.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Aug 21, 2004 3:30 pm Wow, that gives me another reason to give Super Robot Wars a whirl.

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Mon Aug 23, 2004 12:28 pm Maybe this should go in a seperate thread, but since you guys brought it up. What is Super Robot Wars? I think I've heard of it, but not any specifics. It sounds like fun!

[]:             Quote Post by NeonZ » Tue Aug 24, 2004 12:29 pm I hope this won't become too off topic... Super Robot Wars is a Japanese turn based strategy/rpg which uses charachters, mechas and plots from many animes and mangas, joining all their storylines. It's a very old series, which began in the Game Boy, and has spawned sequels for almost all mainstream systems. Regarding Evangelion in SRW, in my honest opnion, it's treatment has always been very good. Evangelion has appeared in SRW F, FF, Alpha and MX. MX seems to be the SRW that is more Eva-like, because three of the series in that game(Eva, RahXephon and Zerorymer) have the same style(though Zeorymer lacks the ending of the world theme). Out of those, I've only played SRW Alpha, which left Eva's storyline fairly intact. "Spoilers ahead" Besides some optional responses, the army being replaced by robots from other series, the lack of some filler angels, an original charachter replacing two other angels, the option of saving Touji, the end of episode 25' and the lack of episode 26', which is replaced by a different ending, the storyline remained close to the anime, the game even covers the incident of Shinji reaching a 400% synch rate and everything that followed that.

[]:             Quote Post by kosure » Tue Aug 24, 2004 5:22 pm It is a really cool scene, although it makes me feel like the manga Rei, and the anime Rei are two completly diffrent people. But I like that, its like two diffrenet Eva series. PS. Notice she's wearing an oversized mans shirt? If Shinji wanted to talk to his dad, maybe he should have checke Rei's bedroom. He was probablly in there. At least until he got his pants on, and climbed out the window. :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Tue Aug 24, 2004 5:48 pm Kosure, that's so funny... :shock: Kosure...you might be on to something... *shudder*

[]:             Quote Post by kosure » Tue Aug 24, 2004 7:12 pm she is Yui,... sorta.

[]: I hear ya...             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Tue Aug 24, 2004 11:40 pm As much as I'd love to discuss this, that's her shirt. Remember what kind of uniform the girl's wear in Tokyo-3 In the naked Rei scene in episode five, Rei starts with her underwear, then puts on the same shirt she is wearing in the manga scene, and puts the skirt thingy on top of that. She just doesn't have any other clothes so when she isn't in uniform, she wears the shirt from her uniform. I agree the manga Rei is a lot different from anime Rei, I also kinda like it that way.

[]: Re: I hear ya...             Quote Post by kosure » Wed Aug 25, 2004 9:38 am Olin of Xephon wrote: As much as I'd love to discuss this, that's her shirt.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Sun Aug 29, 2004 4:28 pm I finally got around to buying volume 5, and I must say, it made the purchase worth it. I really liked the last few lines, that reffered to Gendo. "It's bitter." "Yes, but warm."

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Oct 22, 2004 7:56 am I've just read it and i must admit that this scene was one of the best..and yeah Phaze's right..

[]:             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Fri Oct 22, 2004 11:05 pm I know. But would you put it past Gendou to sleep with her anyway?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Oct 23, 2004 11:47 pm Strangely, the tea scene was one of the "original" manga scenes that annoyed me the most. Faring slightly worse than that with me is the Ritsuko "It was just a joke!" scene. Dear gawd...

[]:             Quote Post by NeonZ » Mon Oct 25, 2004 7:36 pm Reichu wrote: Strangely, the tea scene was one of the "original" manga scenes that annoyed me the most. Faring slightly worse than that with me is the Ritsuko "It was just a joke!" scene. Dear gawd...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Oct 26, 2004 11:26 am NeonZ wrote: I'm not a fan of the tea scene(this topic was created just because I was curious about it in SRW), the whole scene seems too... I dunno... but I don't like it much... Maybe, it was just because Rei showed emotions too suddenly...
